channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center
item.description: The Kennedy Center’s link to Trump has officially been severed. A judge ruled renaming of the historic venue invalid, reinstating its original dedication to John F. Kennedy.

item.title: Peace agreement between Iran and United States can be finalised in 24 hours
item.description: Pakistan prepares for potential peace pact between Iran and US within 24 hours, but new clashes underscore lingering uncertainties.

item.title: Belgian company entrusted with visuals for NBA finals
item.description: Madison Square Garden becomes a canvas for Belgian creators as the Knicks pursue their first NBA finals victory in over two decades.

item.title: Sweden intercepts Russian fighter jets over Baltic Sea
item.description: Russian aircraft tested Sweden’s borders on Friday, prompting swift NATO-aligned responses. The Baltic region remains a flashpoint as tensions surge post-Ukraine invasion.

item.title: Three climbers die in the Italian Alps
item.description: Three mountaineers have died in the Italian Alps, authorities confirmed on Saturday. Three mountaineers have died on the Gran Paradiso Mt. in the Italian Alps, authorities confirmed on Saturday. Rescue teams discovered their bodies at an altitude of about 3,600 metres on the Gran Paradisoeams discovered their bodies at an altitude of about 3,600 metres on the Gran Paradiso

item.title: Iran announces proposed pact aimed at lifting the US blockade
item.description: Iran says an agreement with the United States to lift the blockade of Iranian ports and alter management of the Strait of Hormuz could take place soon.

item.title: EU accession negotiations with Ukraine and Moldova resume on Monday
item.description: EU ambassadors approved the opening on Monday of the first cluster of negotiations on the accession of Ukraine and Moldova to the EU. On Monday,

item.title: United Kingdom: Dan Jarvis named new Defence Secretary
item.description: Dan Jarvis is appointed as the UK Defence Minister following the resignation of his predecessor, John Healey, over disagreements regarding military investment.

item.title: 2026 World Cup: What can we expect from Belgium?
item.description: After crashing out in the group stage last time out, Belgium’s Red Devils are looking to bounce back at this year’s World Cup in North America.

item.title: Two children and one adult die in accident near Belgian border
item.description: The group, consisting of 14 children on bicycles and two adult supervisors, was taking part in a school camp when the accident happened.

item.title: First leather bag from T-Rex cells to be auctioned in Paris
item.description: Advances in biotechnology now allow scientists to instruct cell cultures to "build" genuine skin from an animal that went extinct 67 million years ago.

item.title: Paying to sit next to your children? UK opens investigation against Ryanair
item.description: Ryanair called the investigation "baseless", claiming full compliance with applicable laws.

item.title: Belgian man jailed after groping flight attendant and attempting to enter cockpit
item.description: "The defendant's actions threatened the safety and security of innocent passengers, crew members, and the flight itself."

item.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz, targets two ships after US strikes
item.description: The announcement came in the wake of fresh US strikes on Wednesday night. US President Donald Trump accused Tehran of "mocking" the United States.

item.title: World Cup will be 'greatest event in human history', says FIFA President
item.description: The event has also come under fire due to Iran's participation, visa issues, ticket availability, and for being "the most polluting World Cup ever staged".

item.title: Netherlands faces threat of electricity shortage by 2030
item.description: In all scenarios examined by the grid operator, there will be a power shortage in the Netherlands by 2030.

item.title: Belgium leave for World Cup after five-star triumph over Tunisia
item.description: Goals from Leandro Trossard, Charles De Ketelaere, Kevin De Bruyne, Dodi Lukebakio and Nicolas Raskin contributed to a perfect send-off for the Red Devils as they fly off to the Americas.

item.title: Press association 'concerned' about journalists' visa situation ahead of World Cup
item.description: Iranian and African reporters face denied or limited US visas before the World Cup. AIPS calls the situation "unacceptable" and wants FIFA to intervene.

item.title: Airlines denounce passengers' compensation for delayed flights
item.description: Airlines say EU's delay policy punishes the many for the few as €8 billion costs mount. A reform plan now faces deadlock.

item.title: Agricultural giant France increasingly imports flour
item.description: Flour imports into France have soared 45% in two years, leaving millers warning of potential threats to domestic production and traditions.
